active_ingredients: Docusate sodium 50 mg to 250 mg per capsule/tablet or per dose of syrup (stool softener, e.g., Colace)
indications_and_usage: Uses: relieves occasional constipation (irregularity); generally produces a bowel movement in 12 to 72 hours. It is a stool softener (emollient laxative) that lets more water and fat mix into the stool, making it easier and more comfortable to pass - useful when straining should be avoided (after surgery, childbirth, with hemorrhoids).
contraindications: Do not use if you have stomach pain, nausea, or vomiting, or if you notice a sudden change in bowel habits lasting more than 2 weeks, without asking a doctor.
warnings_and_cautions: Do not use for more than 1 week unless directed by a doctor; laxative dependence can occur with prolonged overuse. Do not take within 2 hours of mineral oil unless directed by a doctor (increases mineral oil absorption). Stop use and ask a doctor if you have rectal bleeding or fail to have a bowel movement - these could indicate a serious condition.
adverse_reactions: Mild abdominal cramping, diarrhea; throat irritation with the liquid; rarely rash.
drug_interactions: May increase the absorption of mineral oil (avoid combining). Generally minimal interactions.
dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 12 years and over: 50-300 mg daily in one or more divided doses until bowel movements are normal; take with a full glass of water. Children 2-11 years: 50-150 mg daily as directed. Children under 2 years: ask a doctor. The syrup can be mixed with milk or juice to improve taste.
overdosage: Overdose may cause abdominal cramping and diarrhea with fluid/electrolyte loss; supportive fluids. Contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222).
